  "concepts": [
    {"id": "ai-readiness", "name": "AI readiness", "aliases": ["AI-ready"], "description": "Human capability to work effectively alongside intelligent systems, integrating functional proficiency, strategic intelligence, ethical stewardship, and critical human skills.", "grounding_span": "readiness as the human capability to work", "location": "Introduction, p.6"},
    {"id": "functional-ai-proficiency", "name": "Functional AI Proficiency", "aliases": ["Functional Proficiency"], "description": "Graduate's ability to use standard AI tools and apply them to professional workflows.", "grounding_span": "Functional AI Proficiency", "location": "Chapter 1, p.21"},
    {"id": "strategic-intelligence", "name": "Strategic Intelligence", "aliases": [], "description": "Ability to identify where AI adds value and where it creates risk within a business context.", "grounding_span": "Strategic Intelligence", "location": "Chapter 1, p.21"},
    {"id": "ethical-stewardship", "name": "Ethical Stewardship", "aliases": [], "description": "Capability to serve as an ethical filter and risk mitigation manager, understanding bias, fairness, and data privacy.", "grounding_span": "Ethical Stewardship", "location": "Chapter 1, p.21"},
    {"id": "critical-human-skills", "name": "Critical Human Skills", "aliases": [], "description": "Human judgment, creativity, communication, collaboration, adaptability, and emotional intelligence AI cannot replicate.", "grounding_span": "Critical Human Skills", "location": "Chapter 1, p.21"},
    {"id": "pace-friction", "name": "Pace Friction", "aliases": ["Pace"], "description": "Gap between the speed of AI-driven workplace change and the slower cadence of curriculum development.", "grounding_span": "Pace Friction", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "connection-friction", "name": "Connection Friction", "aliases": ["Connection"], "description": "Weak feedback loops and partnership infrastructure between employers and institutions.", "grounding_span": "Connection friction:", "location": "Chapter 3, p.36"},
    {"id": "capability-friction", "name": "Capability Friction", "aliases": ["Capability"], "description": "Uneven faculty AI capability limiting consistent integration of AI into learning.", "grounding_span": "Capability friction:", "location": "Chapter 3, p.37"},
    {"id": "governance-friction", "name": "Governance Friction", "aliases": ["Governance"], "description": "Fragmented policies that fail to guide responsible AI use, producing shadow adoption.", "grounding_span": "Governance Friction", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "experience-friction", "name": "Experience Friction", "aliases": ["Experience"], "description": "Disconnect between access to AI tools and structured opportunities to apply them.", "grounding_span": "Experience Friction", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "skills-friction", "name": "Skills Friction", "aliases": ["Skills"], "description": "Misalignment between capabilities graduates demonstrate and what employers require.", "grounding_span": "Skills Friction", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "shadow-ai", "name": "Shadow AI", "aliases": ["shadow AI environment", "shadow adoption"], "description": "Students developing localized AI skills outside any institutional framework or guidance.", "grounding_span": "shadow AI", "location": "Chapter 2, p.29"},
    {"id": "skills-chasm", "name": "Skills chasm", "aliases": [], "description": "Widening gap between university education and entry-level employment requirements.", "grounding_span": "skills chasm", "location": "Introduction, p.5"},
    {"id": "skills-half-life", "name": "Skills half-life", "aliases": [], "description": "The shrinking period before a skill loses relevance, reported as dropping to 2-3 years.", "grounding_span": "skills half-life", "location": "Introduction, p.5"},
    {"id": "curriculum-lottery", "name": "Curriculum lottery", "aliases": [], "description": "Uneven student experience contingent on individual educator AI proficiency.", "grounding_span": "curriculum lottery", "location": "Chapter 3, p.37"},
    {"id": "humans-in-the-lead", "name": "Humans-in-the-lead", "aliases": [], "description": "Philosophy orienting curriculum around graduates who use AI as input to decisions they own.", "grounding_span": "humans-in-the-lead", "location": "Chapter 3, p.40"},
    {"id": "humans-in-the-loop", "name": "Humans-in-the-loop", "aliases": [], "description": "Conventional framing of human oversight of AI systems.", "grounding_span": "humans in the loop", "location": "Chapter 3, p.40"},
    {"id": "authentic-assessment", "name": "Authentic assessment", "aliases": [], "description": "Assessment designed so parameters closely mirror how students will function in a job.", "grounding_span": "authentic assessment", "location": "Chapter 3, p.39"},
    {"id": "t-shaped-graduate", "name": "T-shaped graduate", "aliases": [], "description": "Graduate combining depth (British model) with a broad core (American model).", "grounding_span": "T-shaped", "location": "Chapter 1, p.15"},
    {"id": "ai-ready-graduate", "name": "AI-ready graduate", "aliases": [], "description": "Graduate combining technological fluency with human-AI judgment employers value from day one.", "grounding_span": "AI-ready graduate", "location": "Chapter 1, p.14"},
    {"id": "education-to-work-transition", "name": "Education-to-work transition", "aliases": ["education-to-work pathway"], "description": "The passage from higher education into the professional workforce.", "grounding_span": "education-to-work transition", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "evaluative-judgment", "name": "Evaluative judgment", "aliases": [], "description": "Judgment previously reserved for senior staff now required of graduates.", "grounding_span": "evaluative judgment", "location": "Chapter 1, p.13"},
    {"id": "trust-gap", "name": "Trust gap", "aliases": [], "description": "Gap where educators cannot tell if improved grades reflect learning or reliance on AI.", "grounding_span": "trust gap", "location": "Chapter 2, p.29"},
    {"id": "faculty-capability", "name": "Faculty capability", "aliases": ["faculty AI capability"], "description": "Primary driving force of graduate AI readiness; the frontline determinant of student experience.", "grounding_span": "faculty capability", "location": "Chapter 2, p.26"},
    {"id": "ai-governance", "name": "AI governance", "aliases": [], "description": "Institutional guidance and culture governing responsible AI use.", "grounding_span": "AI governance", "location": "Conclusion, p.45"},
    {"id": "degree-apprenticeships", "name": "Degree apprenticeships", "aliases": [], "description": "Work-integrated learning mechanism for building applied experience.", "grounding_span": "degree apprenticeships", "location": "Conclusion, p.44"},
    {"id": "stackable-credentials", "name": "Stackable credentials", "aliases": [], "description": "Modular credentials enabling faster curriculum adaptation.", "grounding_span": "stackable credentials", "location": "Conclusion, p.44"},
    {"id": "graduate-ai-attribute-framework", "name": "Graduate AI Attribute Framework", "aliases": [], "description": "Suggested institution-employer co-designed framework of graduate AI attributes.", "grounding_span": "Graduate AI Attribute Framework", "location": "Conclusion, p.44"},
    {"id": "co-creation-model", "name": "Connected co-creation model", "aliases": [], "description": "Suggested most impactful approach: educators and employers co-designing learning architectures.", "grounding_span": "co-creation model", "location": "Introduction, p.6"},
    {"id": "applied-readiness", "name": "Applied readiness", "aliases": [], "description": "Readiness proven when learning translates into workplace capability; distinct from mere tool access.", "grounding_span": "applied readiness", "location": "Foreword (Pearson), p.3"},
    {"id": "generative-ai", "name": "Generative AI", "aliases": ["GenAI"], "description": "Generative AI technologies diffusing rapidly in the workplace.", "grounding_span": "generative AI", "location": "Chapter 1, p.12"},
    {"id": "entry-level-roles", "name": "Entry-level roles", "aliases": ["professional stepping stones"], "description": "Early-career roles being automated and redesigned by AI.", "grounding_span": "entry-level roles", "location": "Introduction, p.5"},
    {"id": "investment-archetypes", "name": "Three investment archetypes", "aliases": ["high velocity strategists", "incremental pragmatists", "fragmented adaptation"], "description": "Clustering of markets by institutional AI investment approach.", "grounding_span": "three archetypes", "location": "Chapter 2, p.24"},
    {"id": "institutional-ai-investment", "name": "Institutional AI investment", "aliases": ["university AI investment"], "description": "Scale and focus of an institution's investment in AI.", "grounding_span": "university AI investment", "location": "Chapter 2, p.24"},
    {"id": "perception-gap", "name": "Perception gap", "aliases": [], "description": "Divergence between stakeholder groups' perceptions of university AI readiness.", "grounding_span": "perception gap", "location": "Chapter 1, p.11"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "def-ai-readiness", "term": "AI readiness", "verbatim_text": "the human capability to work effectively alongside intelligent systems: an integration of functional AI proficiency, strategic intelligence, ethical stewardship, and critical human skills such as adaptability, communication, and judgment", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-04", "grounding_span": "readiness as the human capability to work", "location": "Introduction, p.6"},
    {"id": "def-pace-friction", "term": "Pace Friction", "verbatim_text": "The widening gap between the speed of AI-driven workplace change and the slower cadence of curriculum development and institutional decision-making.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-04", "grounding_span": "The widening gap between the speed of", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "def-connection-friction", "term": "Connection Friction", "verbatim_text": "Weak feedback loops between education and employers reduce alignment between workforce needs and learning design.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-04", "grounding_span": "Weak feedback loops between", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "def-capability-friction", "term": "Capability Friction", "verbatim_text": "Uneven faculty and instructor AI capability limits consistent and confident integration of AI into learning experiences.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-04", "grounding_span": "Uneven faculty and instructor AI", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "def-governance-friction", "term": "Governance Friction", "verbatim_text": "The absence of clear, practical guidance for responsible AI use leads to shadow adoption and unmanaged risk entering the workplace.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-04", "grounding_span": "The absence of clear, practical guidance", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "def-experience-friction", "term": "Experience Friction", "verbatim_text": "A disconnect between access to AI tools and structured opportunities to practice, apply, and demonstrate real-world capability.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-04", "grounding_span": "A disconnect between access to AI tools", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "def-skills-friction", "term": "Skills Friction", "verbatim_text": "Misalignment between the capabilities graduates demonstrate and the applied judgment, adaptability, and collaboration employers require in AI-enabled roles.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-04", "grounding_span": "Misalignment between the capabilities", "location": "Chapter 3, p.33"},
    {"id": "def-authentic-assessment", "term": "Authentic assessment", "verbatim_text": "assessment parameters should be very much close to how students will function in the job, that is what we call authentic assessment", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026-04", "grounding_span": "to how students will function in the job, that is what we", "location": "Chapter 3, p.39"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "c1", "claim_text": "Approximately 67% of survey respondents categorize the pace of AI-driven change as extremely or very fast.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "67% of survey respondents categorize the pace", "location": "Chapter 1, p.9"},
    {"id": "c2", "claim_text": "70% of HE leaders expect the pace of AI-driven change to accelerate over the next two years.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "70% of HE leaders say that they expect the pace", "location": "Chapter 2, p.24"},
    {"id": "c3", "claim_text": "Only 24% of stakeholders believe universities are keeping pace with most or all developments.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "only 24% of stakeholders believe", "location": "Chapter 1, p.11"},
    {"id": "c4", "claim_text": "63% of stakeholders view a university degree as more essential than five years ago.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "view the degree as more essential", "location": "Chapter 1, p.13"},
    {"id": "c5", "claim_text": "Inefficient career transitions and learning gaps contribute to annual US losses of about $1.1 trillion, roughly 5% of 2023 GDP.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "roughly 5% of its 2023 gross domestic product", "location": "Introduction, p.5"},
    {"id": "c6", "claim_text": "The WEF projects AI may displace 92 million jobs by 2030 while creating 170 million, a net gain of 78 million.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "AI may displace 92 million jobs by 2030", "location": "Introduction, p.5"},
    {"id": "c7", "claim_text": "Experts report the skills half-life is dropping to 2-3 years.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the ‘skills half-life’ is dropping to 2-3", "location": "Introduction, p.5"},
    {"id": "c8", "claim_text": "The report's findings draw on a survey of 2,711 stakeholders across six countries.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "2,711 stakeholders comprising", "location": "Introduction, p.6"},
    {"id": "c9", "claim_text": "More than 3 in 4 HE leaders (78%) believe their graduates meet employer expectations.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "HE leaders (78%) believe that their graduates", "location": "Chapter 1, p.14"},
    {"id": "c10", "claim_text": "42% of employers cite lack of hands-on experience with workplace tools as a top barrier to hiring.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "a lack of hands-on experience with workplace tools (42%)", "location": "Chapter 1, p.17"},
    {"id": "c11", "claim_text": "Only 13% (1 in 8) of HE leaders characterize their faculty's AI capability as very strong.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Only 13% of HE leaders characterize their", "location": "Chapter 3, p.37"},
    {"id": "c12", "claim_text": "Faculty AI expertise is the #1 barrier to readiness, cited by 40% of HE leaders.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Faculty AI expertise is the #1 barrier to", "location": "Chapter 3, p.37"},
    {"id": "c13", "claim_text": "49% of students use independent tools for writing versus 20% using university tools.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "49% of students use independent", "location": "Chapter 3, p.38"},
    {"id": "c14", "claim_text": "42% of students use independent stacks for coding instead of university tools.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "stacks for coding instead of university", "location": "Chapter 3, p.38"},
    {"id": "c15", "claim_text": "Employers rank graduates' ability to critically verify AI outputs as their weakest competency.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "rank it as graduates’ weakest", "location": "Chapter 1, p.20"},
    {"id": "c16", "claim_text": "Speed is the primary barrier to curriculum adaptation, cited by 42% of HE leaders.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Speed is the primary barrier to", "location": "Chapter 3, p.35"},
    {"id": "c17", "claim_text": "53% of employers report finding graduates with the right skills is their primary challenge, rising to 69% in Malaysia.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "53% of employers report that finding", "location": "Chapter 3, p.35"},
    {"id": "c18", "claim_text": "Only 25% of HE leaders have formal systematic processes to gather workforce needs.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Only 25% of HE leaders say that they", "location": "Chapter 3, p.36"},
    {"id": "c19", "claim_text": "31% of employers report difficulty communicating their specific workforce needs to HE institutions.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "31% of employers report difficulty in", "location": "Chapter 3, p.36"},
    {"id": "c20", "claim_text": "Industry partnerships rank last among HE investment priorities despite employer skills shortages.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "industry partnerships rank last", "location": "Chapter 3, p.36"},
    {"id": "c21", "claim_text": "Generative AI models are estimated to have a functional shelf-life of no more than 24 months.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "functional shelf-life of no more than 24", "location": "Chapter 3, p.35"},
    {"id": "c22", "claim_text": "UCL's IXN delivers approximately 1,500 projects per year, with 40-45% of students recruited by industry partners.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "delivers approximately 1,500 projects per year", "location": "Chapter 3, p.36"},
    {"id": "c23", "claim_text": "The AI Assessment Scale has been adopted by over 350 institutions worldwide.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "350 institutions across the world", "location": "Chapter 3, p.38"},
    {"id": "c24", "claim_text": "Employers rank communication and collaboration (50%) and adaptability (45%) as their highest priorities.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "employers rank communication and collaboration (50%) and adaptability (45%)", "location": "Chapter 1, p.15"},
    {"id": "c25", "claim_text": "Nearly two-thirds of employers (61%) state critical human skills are equally important to functional AI proficiency.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Nearly two-thirds of employers (61%)", "location": "Chapter 1, p.20"},
    {"id": "c26", "claim_text": "Working alongside AI is the least-taught topic in the curriculum.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the least-taught topic in the curriculum", "location": "Chapter 3, p.40"},
    {"id": "c27", "claim_text": "Only 1 in 3 learners (30%) report AI is covered extensively or quite a lot in their curriculum.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "report that AI is covered ‘extensively’ or", "location": "Chapter 2, p.29"},
    {"id": "c28", "claim_text": "UK HE leaders prioritize communication skills at 36% against an employer demand of 63%, a 27-point gap.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "skills at 36% against an employer", "location": "Chapter 3, p.40"}
  ],
  "instruments": [
    {"id": "psb-ai-readiness-survey", "name": "AI Readiness multi-market study (Pearson/AWS)", "owner": "PSB Insights", "year": 2026, "method": "Mixed-methods: online quantitative survey of 2,711 stakeholders across six markets plus 7 qualitative interviews", "grounding_span": "All fieldwork was conducted \nby PSB Insights.", "location": "Methodology, p.49"}
  ],
  "measures": [
    {"id": "me1", "text": "Thinking about how AI is impacting the workplace and workforce requirements, how would you describe the pace of AI-driven change you are currently experiencing?", "response_type": "categorical scale (Extremely/Very/…)", "grounding_span": "describe the pace of AI-driven change you are currently experiencing?", "location": "Chapter 1, p.10"},
    {"id": "me2", "text": "Which of the following statements is closest to your view about higher education in the context of AI?", "response_type": "single-choice", "grounding_span": "Which of the following statements is closest to your view about higher education in the context of AI?", "location": "Chapter 1, p.12"},
    {"id": "me3", "text": "Which of the following statements is closest to your view about preparing for AI-enabled workplaces?", "response_type": "single-choice", "grounding_span": "closest to your view about preparing for", "location": "Chapter 1, p.13"},
    {"id": "me4", "text": "Thinking about how AI is changing workplaces, which type of skills do you believe are becoming MORE VALUABLE?", "response_type": "single-choice", "grounding_span": "which type of skills do you believe are becoming", "location": "Chapter 1, p.14"},
    {"id": "me5", "text": "How would you characterize your institution's current level of investment in AI?", "response_type": "categorical (Significant/Moderate/Limited/Don't know)", "grounding_span": "How would you characterize your institution’s current level of", "location": "Chapter 2, p.24"},
    {"id": "me6", "text": "Overall, how would you assess the AI knowledge and capability of faculty at your institution?", "response_type": "categorical (Very strong…Very weak)", "grounding_span": "how would you assess the AI knowledge and capability of faculty at your institution?", "location": "Chapter 2, p.27"},
    {"id": "me7", "text": "Please imagine a ladder, with steps numbered from 0 at the bottom to 10 at the top … On which step would you say you personally stand right now?", "response_type": "0-10 ladder scale", "grounding_span": "Please imagine a ladder, with steps numbered from 0 at the bottom to 10 at the top.", "location": "Chapter 2, p.28"},
    {"id": "me8", "text": "Are you aware of any university guidance, directives or 'rules' on how to appropriately and effectively use AI to support your studies?", "response_type": "categorical (Yes definitely/Yes maybe/No)", "grounding_span": "Are you aware of any university guidance, directives or ‘rules’ on how to", "location": "Chapter 2, p.30"},
    {"id": "me9", "text": "When you use AI for your university studies, which of the following is closest to your view?", "response_type": "single-choice", "grounding_span": "When you use AI for your university studies, which of the following is", "location": "Chapter 2, p.31"},
    {"id": "me10", "text": "What, if any, are the main barriers your organization faces in hiring or developing graduates for AI-enabled roles?", "response_type": "multi-select", "grounding_span": "What, if any, are the main barriers your organization faces in hiring or developing graduates for AI-enabled roles?", "location": "Chapter 1, p.17"},
    {"id": "me11", "text": "Which THREE of the following are MOST IMPORTANT when hiring graduates into your organization today?", "response_type": "multi-select (choose three)", "grounding_span": "Which THREE of the following are MOST IMPORTANT when hiring graduates into your organization", "location": "Chapter 1, p.16"}
  ],
